content:
How to Apply to University of Venda (UNIVEN)

Thinking about studying at University of Venda (UNIVEN)? Based in Thohoyandou, Limpopo, UNIVEN is a Traditional University established in 1982. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: UNIVEN Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameUniversity of Venda (UNIVEN)
TypeTraditional University
LocationThohoyandou, Limpopo (Venda region)
Year Founded1982
Application Portalhttps://www.univen.ac.za/index.php/admissions
Application FeeR130
Applications OpenApril 1
Applications CloseSeptember 30
Minimum APS26 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone015 962 8000
Contact Emailadmissions@univen.ac.za

Why Choose UNIVEN?

Key institution serving the Vhembe district and Zimbabwe border region.

  • Located in the scenic Venda region, near the Zimbabwe border
  • One of South Africa's most rural-focused universities, serving communities in the Vhembe district
  • Strong in environmental sciences with the nearby Kruger National Park ecosystem
  • Offers unique programmes in indigenous knowledge systems and African languages (Tshivenda)

Faculties and Programmes at UNIVEN

UNIVEN offers qualifications across 8 faculties:

  • Agriculture
  • Education
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Health Sciences
  • Humanities
  • Law
  • Management Sciences
  • Mathematical & Natural Sciences

Popular programmes include: BSc Environmental Management, BA Social Work, LLB, BNursing, BSc Agriculture.

APS Requirements for UNIVEN

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at UNIVEN is 26 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: 26–34 depending on faculty

UNIVEN accepts a minimum APS of 26 for most programmes, ensuring access for students from rural Limpopo. The university offers extended curriculum programmes for students needing extra academic support.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to UNIVEN

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.univen.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the UNIVEN online application portal at https://www.univen.ac.za/index.php/admissions. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R130.

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from UNIVEN, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for UNIVEN

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: April 1
  • Application Deadline: September 30
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at UNIVEN

Studying at UNIVEN can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS
  • UNIVEN Financial Aid
  • Limpopo Provincial Bursaries
  • Venetia Diamond Mine Bursary

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for UNIVEN While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at UNIVEN to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to UNIVEN, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing UNIVEN With Other Universities

Not sure if UNIVEN is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorUNIVENTypical Alternative
TypeTraditional UniversityTraditional University / UoT
LocationThohoyandouVaries
Minimum APS2620–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR130R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to UNIVEN

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to UNIVEN.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet UNIVEN's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.
keywords: University of Venda (UNIVEN) how to apply
quickAnswer: To apply to University of Venda (UNIVEN), visit https://www.univen.ac.za/index.php/admissions, pay the R130 application fee, and submit your documents before September 30. Minimum APS is 26 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to UNIVEN?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at UNIVEN is 26 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (26–34 depending on faculty). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the UNIVEN website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for UNIVEN?
faqs.answer: Applications for UNIVEN typically open in April 1 and close on September 30. We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.univen.ac.za/index.php/admissions to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for UNIVEN?
faqs.answer: The application fee at UNIVEN is R130. Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the UNIVEN website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does UNIVEN accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, UNIVEN is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to UNIVEN?
faqs.answer: For your application to UNIVEN, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
